encounters.scenario_text: 78-year-old male POD #2 from sigmoid resection. Vitals trending — last set: T 38.7°C, HR 122, BP 84/52, RR 26, SpO2 91% on 2 L NC. Lactate 4.2 just back from the lab. Patient now hyperactive — picking at his IV, paranoid about staff entering the room. Wife at bedside crying. Charge nurse paged for assistance. Pre-existing COPD.

objective_alignment.summary: Sepsis (suspected infection + organ dysfunction), septic shock (vasopressor-requiring hypotension + lactate>2), Hour-1 bundle, qSOFA, fluid resuscitation, source control.

medications.label: Medications
all_items: broad-spectrum antibiotics within 1 hour (vancomycin + cefepime/piperacillin-tazobactam typical)
unmatched: broad-spectrum antibiotics within 1 hour (vancomycin + cefepime/piperacillin-tazobactam typical)

procedures.label: Procedures
all_items: Hour-1 bundle: lactate, blood cultures BEFORE antibiotics, antibiotics, 30 mL/kg fluids if hypotensive or lactate>4, pressors for MAP<65 after fluids
source control (drain abscess, remove infected line)
unmatched: Hour-1 bundle: lactate, blood cultures BEFORE antibiotics, antibiotics, 30 mL/kg fluids if hypotensive or lactate>4, pressors for MAP<65 after fluids
source control (drain abscess, remove infected line)

redFlags.label: Red flags
all_items: peripheral hypoperfusion + warm extremities (early) → cold + mottled (late)
unmatched: peripheral hypoperfusion + warm extremities (early) → cold + mottled (late)

role_hooks.Patient: I just feel really off, like I'm not myself. I'm cold but the nurse says I have a fever.
role_hooks.Family: He was fine yesterday. Now he doesn't even know who I am.
role_hooks.Clinician: Get me a lactate and blood cultures off two sites before we hang the antibiotics. Then 30 per kg of LR wide open.
role_hooks.AlliedHealth: Lab here — preliminary cultures at 24 hours show gram-positive cocci in clusters. I've called the team.

scope_of_practice.LPN: Recognize early sepsis signs (fever/hypothermia, tachycardia, AMS), report immediately, support fluid administration and monitoring.
scope_of_practice.RN: Initiate sepsis bundle, draw cultures, hang antibiotics, manage fluids and pressors per protocol, monitor lactate trend, escalate to ICU.
scope_of_practice.Advanced: Diagnose, order workup, manage vasopressors, lead ICU resuscitation, source control, antibiotic stewardship.

objective_alignment.summary: Acute vs. chronic pain, multimodal analgesia, opioid stewardship, PCA, epidural, non-pharmacologic methods, pain in special populations.

medications.label: Medications
all_items: acetaminophen (max 3-4 g/day, lower in liver disease)
opioids: morphine, hydromorphone, fentanyl, oxycodone, hydrocodone
adjuvants: gabapentin, pregabalin, duloxetine, amitriptyline, capsaicin
unmatched: acetaminophen (max 3-4 g/day, lower in liver disease)
opioids: morphine, hydromorphone, fentanyl, oxycodone, hydrocodone
adjuvants: gabapentin, pregabalin, duloxetine, amitriptyline, capsaicin

procedures.label: Procedures
all_items: 0-10 numeric pain scale, FLACC (peds), PAINAD (dementia), Wong-Baker faces
PCA loading dose, basal rate, demand bolus
naloxone administration: 0.04-0.4 mg IV/IM/SC, repeat q2-3 min, watch for re-narcotization
unmatched: 0-10 numeric pain scale, FLACC (peds), PAINAD (dementia), Wong-Baker faces
PCA loading dose, basal rate, demand bolus
naloxone administration: 0.04-0.4 mg IV/IM/SC, repeat q2-3 min, watch for re-narcotization

redFlags.label: Red flags
all_items: pinpoint pupils with sedation = opioid toxicity
opioid withdrawal: yawning, lacrimation, mydriasis, piloerection, GI symptoms
unmatched: pinpoint pupils with sedation = opioid toxicity
opioid withdrawal: yawning, lacrimation, mydriasis, piloerection, GI symptoms

role_hooks.Patient: My pain is 10/10 and you're not listening.
role_hooks.Family: Why isn't anyone giving him anything? He's clearly hurting.
role_hooks.Clinician: Has she had her q4 dose? Let's add a scheduled NSAID and reassess.
role_hooks.AlliedHealth: Pharmacy — for renal/hepatic dose adjustment; PT for movement strategies; spiritual care for total pain.

scope_of_practice.LPN: Reassess pain after intervention, administer ordered analgesics by approved route. Cannot manage PCA programming or epidural in most states. Recognize and report respiratory depression.
scope_of_practice.RN: Administer IV opioids, manage PCA, monitor epidural, titrate per protocol, administer naloxone, lead pain reassessment, request order changes.
scope_of_practice.Advanced: Order regional anesthesia, manage acute pain service, prescribe controlled substances per DEA registration, initiate buprenorphine.

objective_alignment.summary: Therapeutic communication: open-ended questions, reflection, silence, validation. SBAR handoff. Cultural humility. Trauma-informed care. De-escalation.

conditions.label: Conditions
all_items: transfer to higher level of care
unmatched: transfer to higher level of care

redFlags.label: Red flags
all_items: yes/no answers from a patient who normally elaborates
unmatched: yes/no answers from a patient who normally elaborates

role_hooks.Patient: I appreciate you taking the time to listen. Most nurses just rush in.
role_hooks.Family: I just want someone to actually tell me what is happening.
role_hooks.Clinician: Give me an SBAR — situation, background, assessment, recommendation. Don't bury the lead.
role_hooks.AlliedHealth: I'll need a quick handoff before I take them to imaging — anything I should know?

scope_of_practice.LPN: Conduct routine communication, document, escalate concerns to RN. Cannot independently lead complex goals-of-care discussions.
scope_of_practice.RN: Lead SBAR handoff, conduct patient/family teaching, initiate goals-of-care conversations within scope, escalate to provider.
scope_of_practice.Advanced: Lead family meetings, deliver bad news, coordinate goals-of-care with palliative/social work.

objective_alignment.summary: Standard precautions, transmission-based isolation (contact, droplet, airborne), PPE donning/doffing order, hand hygiene, sharps safety, environmental cleaning.

medications.label: Medications
all_items: soap (C. diff requires soap+water, not ABHR)
bleach wipes for C. diff rooms
unmatched: soap (C. diff requires soap+water, not ABHR)
bleach wipes for C. diff rooms

procedures.label: Procedures
all_items: PPE doffing sequence: gloves → goggles → gown → mask (last)
hand hygiene before donning + after doffing
unmatched: PPE doffing sequence: gloves → goggles → gown → mask (last)
hand hygiene before donning + after doffing

redFlags.label: Red flags
all_items: needlestick injury → immediate report + PEP if indicated
unmatched: needlestick injury → immediate report + PEP if indicated

role_hooks.Patient: Why is everyone wearing all that gear when they come in?
role_hooks.Family: Can I still touch him? Is it dangerous for me?
role_hooks.Clinician: Confirm the isolation type before you go in — droplet vs. contact changes the PPE.
role_hooks.AlliedHealth: I need to know the isolation status before I bring the meal tray in.

scope_of_practice.LPN: Apply standard + transmission-based precautions, use PPE correctly, recognize and report breaches.
scope_of_practice.RN: Initiate isolation orders within protocol, educate patient/family/visitors, coordinate with infection prevention.
scope_of_practice.Advanced: Adjust isolation status, manage outbreak response, lead institutional infection-prevention initiatives.

objective_alignment.summary: Pharmacology principles: pharmacokinetics, pharmacodynamics, dose calculation, weight-based dosing, renal/hepatic adjustment, high-alert medications, look-alike-sound-alike pairs.

medications.label: Medications
all_items: high-alert: insulin, heparin, opioids, chemotherapy, concentrated electrolytes (KCl)
unmatched: high-alert: insulin, heparin, opioids, chemotherapy, concentrated electrolytes (KCl)

procedures.label: Procedures
all_items: five rights (right patient, drug, dose, route, time) + extended (documentation, indication, response)
med math: D/H × Q, weight-based mg/kg, drip rate calculation
unmatched: five rights (right patient, drug, dose, route, time) + extended (documentation, indication, response)
med math: D/H × Q, weight-based mg/kg, drip rate calculation

redFlags.label: Red flags
all_items: look-alike pair (e.g., hydromorphone vs. morphine) confused
unmatched: look-alike pair (e.g., hydromorphone vs. morphine) confused

role_hooks.Patient: Wait — that's a different color than my usual pill. Is that the same thing?
role_hooks.Family: He's on so many medications now. What are these for?
role_hooks.Clinician: Run the math on me before you push anything high-alert.
role_hooks.AlliedHealth: Pharmacy here — that combination has a known QT-prolongation interaction. I'd choose an alternative.

scope_of_practice.LPN: Calculate and administer routine ordered medications by approved routes; cannot administer IV push opioids in most states.
scope_of_practice.RN: Administer all routes, IV push, titrate per protocol, manage smart pumps, perform independent double-checks.
scope_of_practice.Advanced: Prescribe, adjust based on labs, manage anticoagulation/insulin titration protocols.

objective_alignment.summary: Routes of administration (PO, SL, buccal, IM, SC, IV, topical, transdermal, inhaled, rectal). Six rights. NG/G-tube administration. Crushing/splitting rules. Z-track. Insulin and heparin SC technique.

devices.label: Devices
all_items: Z-track needle technique for IM iron
unmatched: Z-track needle technique for IM iron

procedures.label: Procedures
all_items: five rights at the bedside, not at the cart
Z-track for IM iron and other staining/irritant meds
unmatched: five rights at the bedside, not at the cart
Z-track for IM iron and other staining/irritant meds

role_hooks.Patient: I can't swallow this with the tube — does it have to be the whole pill?
role_hooks.Family: Is there a liquid version of his blood pressure med?
role_hooks.Clinician: Hold that — confirm the route before you give it. The order said SL, not PO.
role_hooks.AlliedHealth: Pharmacy can compound a suspension if the patient can't swallow tablets — let me know.

scope_of_practice.LPN: PO, SC, IM, topical, ophthalmic, otic, NG, rectal. Many states: cannot give IV push or initial IV piggyback.
scope_of_practice.RN: All routes including IV push and titrated drips per protocol.
scope_of_practice.Advanced: Order, adjust, prescribe controlled substances per DEA registration.

transcript.text:
*eyes dart toward you, then away*

Who—? Are you... did they send you? My IV's got something wrong with it, I can feel it, it's—

*tugs at blanket restlessly*

What time is it? Where's my... I need to get up, I need to—there's something I'm supposed to do.
you're supposed to meet with Dr Feelgood

*head snaps toward you, pupils dilated*

Dr—? No, no, that's not... when did he say that? 

*fidgets with the bed rail*

I don't remember that. My wife, where's—is she still here? Everything's moving too fast. One second it's morning, the next—

*scratches at arm*

Wait, what did he want? Why does everyone keep coming in and out, in and out—

encounters.scenario_text: 68-year-old male admitted with community-acquired pneumonia now in acute hypoxemic respiratory failure. SpO2 86% on 6 L NC, RR 32, accessory muscle use, mild confusion. Respiratory therapy at bedside, BiPAP being set up. ABG pending. Considering ICU transfer. Practice oxygen escalation, ABG interpretation, criteria for higher level of care, closed-loop with RT and provider.

objective_alignment.summary: Routes of administration (PO, SL, buccal, IM, SC, IV, topical, transdermal, inhaled, rectal). Six rights. NG/G-tube administration. Crushing/splitting rules. Z-track. Insulin and heparin SC technique.

reactions.prompt: How did this run go for each of you?

analysis.prompt: What did you notice? What surprised you?

application.prompt: What will you do differently next shift?
